children.label: A
children.text: the recomputed basis of the property, or

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a sale, exchange, or involuntary conversion, the amount realized, or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of any other disposition, the fair market value of such property,

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “recomputed basis” means, with respect to any property, its adjusted basis recomputed by adding thereto all adjustments reflected in such adjusted basis on account of deductions (whether in respect of the same or other property) allowed or allowable to the taxpayer or to any other person for depreciation or amortization.

children.heading: Taxpayer may establish amount allowed
children.label: B Taxpayer may establish amount allowed
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), if the taxpayer can establish by adequate records or other sufficient evidence that the amount allowed for depreciation or amortization for any period was less than the amount allowable, the amount added for such period shall be the amount allowed.

children.heading: Certain deductions treated as amortization
children.label: C Certain deductions treated as amortization
children.text: Any deduction allowable under section 179, 179B, 179C, 179D, 179E, 181, 190, 193, or 194 shall be treated as if it were a deduction allowable for amortization.

children.label: A
children.text: personal property,

children.label: i
children.text: was used as an integral part of manufacturing, production, or extraction or of furnishing transportation, communications, electrical energy, gas, water, or sewage disposal services,

children.label: ii
children.text: constituted a research facility used in connection with any of the activities referred to in clause (i), or

children.label: iii
children.text: constituted a facility used in connection with any of the activities referred to in clause (i) for the bulk storage of fungible commodities (including commodities in a liquid or gaseous state),

children.label: C
children.text: so much of any real property (other than any property described in subparagraph (B)) which has an adjusted basis in which there are reflected adjustments for amortization under section 169, 179, 179B, 179C, 179D, 179E, 188 (as in effect before its repeal by the Revenue Reconciliation Act of 1990), 190, 193, or 1941So in original. Probably should be followed by a comma.1

children.label: D
children.text: a single purpose agricultural or horticultural structure (as defined in section 168(i)(13)),

children.label: E
children.text: a storage facility (not including a building or its structural components) used in connection with the distribution of petroleum or any primary product of petroleum,

children.label: F
children.text: any railroad grading or tunnel bore (as defined in section 168(e)(4)), or

children.label: G
children.text: any qualified production property (as defined in section 168(n)(2)).

children.heading: Gifts
children.label: 1 Gifts
children.text: Subsection (a) shall not apply to a disposition by gift.

children.heading: Transfers at death
children.label: 2 Transfers at death
children.text: Except as provided in section 691 (relating to income in respect of a decedent), subsection (a) shall not apply to a transfer at death.

children.heading: Certain tax-free transactions
children.label: 3 Certain tax-free transactions
children.text: If the basis of property in the hands of a transferee is determined by reference to its basis in the hands of the transferor by reason of the application of section 332, 351, 361, 721, or 731, then the amount of gain taken into account by the transferor under subsection (a)(1) shall not exceed the amount of gain recognized to the transferor on the transfer of such property (determined without regard to this section). Except as provided in paragraph (6), this paragraph shall not apply to a disposition to an organization (other than a cooperative described in section 521) which is exempt from the tax imposed by this chapter.

children.heading: Like kind exchanges; involuntary conversions, etc.
children.label: 4 Like kind exchanges; involuntary conversions, etc.
A
children.text: the amount of gain recognized on such disposition (determined without regard to this section), plus

children.label: B
children.text: the fair market value of property acquired which is not section 1245 property and which is not taken into account under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Property distributed by a partnership to a partner
children.label: 5 Property distributed by a partnership to a partner

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of this section, the basis of section 1245 property distributed by a partnership to a partner shall be deemed to be determined by reference to the adjusted basis of such property to the partnership.

children.label: i
children.text: the amount of the gain to which subsection (a) would have applied if such property had been sold by the partnership immediately before the distribution at its fair market value at such time, reduced by

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of such gain to which section 751(b) applied.

children.heading: Transfers to tax-exempt organization where property will be used in unrelated business
children.label: 6 Transfers to tax-exempt organization where property will be used in unrelated business

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The second sentence of paragraph (3) shall not apply to a disposition of section 1245 property to an organization described in section 511(a)(2) or 511(b)(2) if, immediately after such disposition, such organization uses such property in an unrelated trade or business (as defined in section 513).

children.heading: Later change in use
children.label: B Later change in use
children.text: If any property with respect to the disposition of which gain is not recognized by reason of subparagraph (A) ceases to be used in an unrelated trade or business of the organization acquiring such property, such organization shall be treated for purposes of this section as having disposed of such property on the date of such cessation.

children.heading: Timber property
children.label: 7 Timber property
children.text: In determining, under subsection (a)(2), the recomputed basis of property with respect to which a deduction under section 194 was allowed for any taxable year, the taxpayer shall not take into account adjustments under section 194 to the extent such adjustments are attributable to the amortizable basis of the taxpayer acquired before the 10th taxable year preceding the taxable year in which gain with respect to the property is recognized.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If a taxpayer disposes of more than 1 amortizable section 197 intangible (as defined in section 197(c)) in a transaction or a series of related transactions, all such amortizable 197 intangibles shall be treated as 1 section 1245 property for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any amortizable section 197 intangible (as so defined) with respect to which the adjusted basis exceeds the fair market value.

children.heading: Adjustments to basis
children.label: c Adjustments to basis
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as he may deem necessary to provide for adjustments to the basis of property to reflect gain recognized under subsection (a).

children.heading: Application of section
children.label: d Application of section
children.text: This section shall apply notwithstanding any other provision of this subtitle.
